metadata.title: Build the Three-File System with Claude: AGENTS + DESIGN + PROJECT
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: The simulation Codex builds without three files is generic. The one built with them is yours.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The simulation Codex builds without three files is generic. The one built with them is yours.

beats.narration_text: The three-file system constrains Codex before Code Mode runs. AGENTS.md: operational rules and never-rules. DESIGN.md: exactly 6 CSS color variables, 2 typefaces, 4 allowed interactions, 5 forbidden interactions. PROJECT.md Layer 1: intent, pedagogical goal, scope boundary. Together they define the solution space before a line of code is generated.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The three-file system constrains Codex before Code Mode runs. AGENTS.md: operational rules and never-rules. DESIGN.md: exactly 6 CSS color variables, 2 typeface

beats.narration_text: Generate DESIGN.md for an interactive bubble-sort simulator: 6 CSS variables, 2 typefaces, 4 allowed interactions, 5 never interactions. Under 80 lines.

props.command: claude "Generate DESIGN.md for an interactive bubble-sort simulator. Color system: exactly 6 CSS variables with hex values and plain-English purpose labels. Typography: 2 faces only. Interaction vocabulary: list 4 ALLOWED interactions and 5 NEVER interactions. Accessibility requirements. Keep under 80 lines."

beats.narration_text: Read it before trusting it. Six color variables with hex and purpose labels, two typefaces, four allowed, five never interactions.

props.code:
## Color system (exactly 6 variables)
--color-background: #FFFFFF  /* page ground */
--color-surface:    #F5F5F5  /* card / panel */
--color-accent:     #C8102E  /* active bar */
--color-text-primary: #2A1A0E  /* headings */
--color-text-secondary: #545454 /* body */
--color-highlight:  #F6D8DC  /* swap flash */

## Typography (2 faces only)
- system-ui — UI labels and controls
- monospace — index values and counts

## ALLOWED interactions (4)
1. Click: step forward one swap
2. Click: reset to initial array
3. Hover bar: show current value tooltip
4. Keyboard: Space to step, R to reset

## NEVER (5)
1. Drag bars  2. Scroll-to-reveal  3. Swipe
4. Right-click context menu  5. Double-click

beats.narration_text: DESIGN.md generated: exactly 6 CSS variables, 2 typefaces, 4 allowed, 5 never interactions. 74 lines — under 80. Three-file system complete under 45 minutes from first prompt.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: DESIGN.md generated: exactly 6 CSS variables, 2 typefaces, 4 allowed, 5 never interactions. 74 lines — under 80. Three-file system complete under 45 minutes fro

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to intentionally violate one DESIGN.md constraint in a sample CSS snippet, then catch and correct it.

props.command: claude "Write a CSS snippet for the bubble-sort simulator that adds an --color-error variable for validation states. Then check it against DESIGN.md and correct any violations."

beats.narration_text: Claude generates a snippet with a seventh color variable — not in the DESIGN.md system. On catch-and-correct, Claude correctly identifies the violation and replaces it with the closest defined variable, with a note explaining the substitution. The DESIGN.md acted as a constraint, not a suggestion.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Claude generates a snippet with a seventh color variable — not in the DESIGN.md system. On catch-and-correct, Claude correctly identifies the violation and repl

beats.narration_text: Six colors is not a limitation — it is the decision that makes the simulation visually coherent. The DESIGN.md that specifies everything gets ignored. The one that specifies the small set of things you actually care about gets followed. Constraints work because they narrow the solution space to the decisions you made.
